```{r}
  filter(!is.na(KEGG_ID), KEGG_ID != "", !is.na(KEGG_Subcategory)) %>%
  mutate(.sub = str_replace_all(as.character(KEGG_Subcategory), "\\s*;\\s*", ";")) %>%
  separate_rows(.sub, sep = ";") %>%
  mutate(Label = str_trim(.sub)) %>%
  inner_join(module_map, by = "Label") %>%
  filter(Module %in% modules_focus) %>%
  distinct(Species_ID, Group_For_Plots, KEGG_ID, Module) %>%
  mutate(
    DataLayer = "Genes",
    Group_For_Plots = factor(Group_For_Plots, levels = groups_use),
    Module = factor(Module, levels = modules_focus)
  )

# -------------------------
# 1B) PROTEINS detected long table
# -------------------------
prot_expr <- protein_table %>%
  mutate(detected = Protein_Abundance_IBAQ_Norm > 0) %>%
  group_by(Species_ID, Protein_ID, Group_For_Plots) %>%
  summarise(
    n_dates_detected = sum(detected, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(Group_For_Plots %in% groups_use) %>%
  filter(n_dates_detected >= detection_filter)

prot_expr_kegg <- prot_expr %>%
  left_join(
    gene_table %>% distinct(Species_ID, Protein_ID, KEGG_ID, KEGG_Subcategory),
    by = c("Species_ID","Protein_ID")
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(KEGG_ID), KEGG_ID != "", !is.na(KEGG_Subcategory))

proteins_long <- prot_expr_kegg %>%
  mutate(.sub = str_replace_all(as.character(KEGG_Subcategory), "\\s*;\\s*", ";")) %>%
  separate_rows(.sub, sep = ";") %>%
  mutate(Label = str_trim(.sub)) %>%
  inner_join(module_map, by = "Label") %>%
  filter(Module %in% modules_focus) %>%
  distinct(Species_ID, Group_For_Plots, KEGG_ID, Module) %>%
  mutate(
    DataLayer = paste0("Proteins detected (≥", detection_filter, ")"),
    Group_For_Plots = factor(Group_For_Plots, levels = groups_use),
    Module = factor(Module, levels = modules_focus)
  )

# Combine both layers into one table
long_all <- bind_rows(genes_long, proteins_long) %>%
  mutate(
    DataLayer = factor(DataLayer, levels = c("Genes", paste0("Proteins detected (≥", detection_filter, ")")))
  )

# ============================================================
# 2) Build union-level similarity matrices -> long table
# ============================================================

make_union_heat_df <- function(df_long, layer_name, mod){

  df_mod <- df_long %>%
    filter(DataLayer == layer_name, Module == mod) %>%
    group_by(Group_For_Plots) %>%
    summarise(kegg_set = list(unique(KEGG_ID)), .groups="drop")

  # Ensure every group exists (even if empty) to avoid [[1]] errors
  df_mod <- tibble(Group_For_Plots = factor(groups_use, levels = groups_use)) %>%
    left_join(df_mod, by = "Group_For_Plots") %>%
    mutate(kegg_set = lapply(kegg_set, function(x) if (is.null(x)) character(0) else x))

  mat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow=length(groups_use), ncol=length(groups_use),
                dimnames=list(groups_use, groups_use))

  for(g1 in groups_use){
    for(g2 in groups_use){
      set1 <- df_mod$kegg_set[df_mod$Group_For_Plots == g1][[1]]
      set2 <- df_mod$kegg_set[df_mod$Group_For_Plots == g2][[1]]
      mat[g1,g2] <- sorensen(set1, set2)
    }
  }

  as.data.frame(as.table(mat)) %>%
    rename(Group1=Var1, Group2=Var2, Similarity=Freq) %>%
    mutate(
      Module = mod,
      DataLayer = layer_name
    )
}

layer_levels <- levels(long_all$DataLayer)

heat_df <- bind_rows(lapply(layer_levels, function(L) {
  bind_rows(lapply(modules_focus, function(M) make_union_heat_df(long_all, L, M)))
})) %>%
  mutate(
    DataLayer = factor(DataLayer, levels = layer_levels),
    Module = factor(Module, levels = modules_focus),
    Group1 = factor(Group1, levels = groups_use),
    Group2 = factor(Group2, levels = groups_use),
    Label  = ifelse(is.na(Similarity), "NA", sprintf("%.2f", Similarity))
  )

# ============================================================
# 3) Plot: 2 rows (Genes top, Proteins bottom) x 3 modules
# ============================================================

p_heat_2rows <- ggplot(heat_df, aes(x = Group1, y = Group2, fill = Similarity)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Label), size = 4.1, fontface = "bold") +
  facet_grid(DataLayer ~ Module) +   # <-- THIS makes 2 rows x 3 columns
  coord_fixed() +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    limits = c(0, 1),
    breaks = c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1),
    name = "Sørensen\nsimilarity"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1),
    axis.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Overlap of KEGG repertoires across trophic strategies",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Union-level Sørensen similarity within each functional module.\n",
      "Proteins detected = ≥", detection_filter, " detection dates (Protein_Abundance_IBAQ_Norm > 0)."
    )
  )

print(p_heat_2rows)

# ggsave(
#   filename = "Fig_Main_Heatmaps_Genes_vs_Proteins_Sorensen.pdf",
#   plot     = p_heat_2rows,
#   width    = 10.8,
#   height   = 6.8,
#   units    = "in",
#   dpi      = 300
# )
# 
# ggsave(
#   filename = "Fig_Main_Heatmaps_Genes_vs_Proteins_Sorensen.png",
#   plot     = p_heat_2rows,
#   width    = 10.8,
#   height   = 6.8,
#   units    = "in",
#   dpi      = 400
# )
```
```{r}
# ============================================================
# NATURE-READY COMPRESSED DUMBBELL
# - 3 rows: Oligo–Intermediate, Intermediate–Copio, Copio–Oligo
# - 3 colored modules per row (slightly offset)
# - open circle = Genes similarity
# - filled circle = Proteins similarity
# - segment color = Δ (Genes − Proteins) magnitude (bigger = darker)
# - BIG dots, clean theme, publication margins
# ============================================================

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(ggplot2)
})

# ---- Palette for modules (your pal3) ----
pal3 <- c(
  "Core metabolism/biosynthesis" = "#E69F00",
  "Resource acquisition"         = "#009E73",
  "Growth machinery"             = "#CC79A7"
)

# ---- DataLayer names must match your heat_df ----
gene_name <- "Genes"
prot_name <- paste0("Proteins detected (≥", detection_filter, ")")

# ---- 3 unique comparisons in your requested order ----
pairs_keep <- tibble(
  A = c("Oligo_like", "Intermediate", "Copio_like"),
  B = c("Intermediate", "Copio_like", "Oligo_like"),
  Pair = factor(
    c("Oligo–Intermediate", "Intermediate–Copio", "Copio–Oligo"),
    levels = c("Copio–Oligo", "Intermediate–Copio", "Oligo–Intermediate")
  )
) %>%
  mutate(Gmin = pmin(A, B), Gmax = pmax(A, B)) %>%
  select(Gmin, Gmax, Pair)

# ---- 1) Filter + make direction-agnostic + collapse duplicates ----
heat_pairs <- heat_df %>%
  mutate(
    Gmin = pmin(as.character(Group1), as.character(Group2)),
    Gmax = pmax(as.character(Group1), as.character(Group2))
  ) %>%
  inner_join(pairs_keep, by = c("Gmin", "Gmax")) %>%
  filter(Module %in% names(pal3)) %>%
  group_by(Module, Pair, DataLayer) %>%
  summarise(Similarity = mean(as.numeric(Similarity), na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# ---- 2) Wide: Genes + Proteins per Module × Pair ----
df_wide <- heat_pairs %>%
  filter(DataLayer %in% c(gene_name, prot_name)) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = DataLayer,
    values_from = Similarity,
    values_fn   = mean
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Module = factor(Module, levels = names(pal3)),
    Pair   = factor(Pair, levels = levels(pairs_keep$Pair)),
    Delta  = .data[[gene_name]] - .data[[prot_name]],
    AbsDelta = abs(Delta)
  )

# ---- 3) Slight vertical offsets so lines don't overlap within each row ----
offset_map <- tibble(
  Module = factor(names(pal3), levels = names(pal3)),
  y_off  = c(+0.22, 0, -0.22)  # Core up, Resource middle, Growth down
)

df_plot <- df_wide %>%
  left_join(offset_map, by = "Module") %>%
  mutate(
    y_base = as.numeric(Pair),
    y      = y_base + y_off
  )

# ---- 4) Nature-style plot ----
pdot <- 8  # BIG dots
pstroke <- 1

p_compact_nature <- ggplot(df_plot) +
  # Segment (colored by delta magnitude)
  geom_segment(
    aes(
      x = .data[[prot_name]], xend = .data[[gene_name]],
      y = y, yend = y,
      alpha = AbsDelta
    ),
    linewidth = 1.5,
    lineend = "round",
    color = "black"
  ) +
  # Proteins point (filled, module-colored)
  geom_point(
    aes(x = .data[[prot_name]], y = y, fill = Module),
    size = pdot,
    shape = 21,
    color = "black",
    stroke = pstroke
  ) +
  # Genes point (open, module-colored outline)
  geom_point(
    aes(x = .data[[gene_name]], y = y, color = Module),
    size = pdot,
    shape = 21,
    fill = "white",
    stroke = pstroke
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal3, guide = guide_legend(order = 1)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal3, guide = "none") +
  # Delta magnitude mapped to alpha (no legend to keep clean)
  scale_alpha_continuous(range = c(0.25, 0.95), guide = "none") +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq_along(levels(df_plot$Pair)),
    labels = levels(df_plot$Pair),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.18, 0.18))
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(0.25, 1, by = 0.25),
    limits = c(0.25, 1)
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 6)),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.key.width = unit(1.1, "lines"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11),
    plot.margin = margin(6, 10, 6, 10)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Sørensen similarity",
    title = "Gene content vs expressed proteins across trophic strategies",
    subtitle = "Open circles = genes; filled circles = proteins detected. Connector darkness scales with |Genes − Proteins|."
  )

print(p_compact_nature)

# Optional save:
ggsave("Fig_Dumbbell_Compact_NatureReady.pdf", p_compact_nature,
       width = 8.2, height = 6, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```
